embed.title: !track
embed.description: Alerts for specific pokemon spawns. The most flexible command — supports IV, CP, level, stat, PVP, form, size, gender, distance, and area filters.

fields.name: Basic usage
fields.value:
`{{prefix}}track pikachu`
`{{prefix}}track pikachu charmander squirtle bulbasaur d:500`
`{{prefix}}track everything iv:100 d:1000` — every 100% spawn within 1km

fields.name: IV / CP / level (ranges are preferred over min/max)
fields.value:
`iv:90-100` — between 90% and 100% IV
`cp:1500-2500` — between 1500 and 2500 CP
`level:25-35` — only level 25 to 35
`atk:13-15 def:13-15 sta:13-15` — individual IV ranges

*Tip: always prefer `iv:90-100` over `miniv:90 maxiv:100`. Both work, the range form reads better and is easier to edit later.*

fields.name: PVP ranks
fields.value:
`great:1-10` — Great league rank 1-10
`ultra:1-10` — Ultra league rank 1-10
`little:1-10` — Little league rank 1-10

Combine multiple leagues in one command:
`{{prefix}}track pikachu great:1-10 ultra:1-10`

`greatcp:1400` sets a minimum league CP floor (often used together with `great:1-10`).

fields.name: Forms
fields.value:
`form:alolan`, `form:galarian`, `form:hisuian`, `form:paldean`
`form:shadow`, `form:purified` — team rocket shadows
`form:santa`, `form:halloween`, etc. — costumed forms

Example: `{{prefix}}track pidgey form:alolan`

fields.name: Distance and area
fields.value:
`d:1000` — within 1000 metres of your `!location`
`area:sw_london` — only inside a specific geofence area (see `{{prefix}}help area`)

If you set neither, alerts come from every area you've added via `{{prefix}}area`.

fields.name: Message flags
fields.value:
`clean` — auto-delete the alert when the pokemon despawns
`edit` — keep one message and edit it in place as the pokemon's data changes
`ping` — @-mention you when the alert fires
`template:2` — use a specific DTS template (see `{{prefix}}info templates`)

fields.name: Remove tracking
fields.value:
`{{prefix}}untrack pikachu` — remove all pikachu tracking
`{{prefix}}track remove pikachu` — same as above
`{{prefix}}untrack id:45` — remove one specific entry by ID (see `{{prefix}}tracked`)
